description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a tan and orange color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, high-mounted wings supported by struts and wires. The fuselage is robust and rounded, with a forward-mounted open cockpit and visible propeller at the nose. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer adorned with insignia and a horizontal stabilizer, both painted in matching colors. The wings display roundels and cross insignia, suggesting a historical military or reconnaissance role. Twin engines are mounted between the wings, each with a visible propeller. The landing gear appears fixed and robust, suitable for unprepared surfaces.
description: A vintage tan and orange biplane with twin engines, high-mounted straight wings, and a single vertical tail fin, featuring military insignia and an open cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rounded fuselage, high-mounted wings, and single vertical tail fin with insignia.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin engines between the wings, open cockpit, and propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the cockpit and tail details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of the biplane's wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane with two straight, parallel wings supported by struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted open cockpit, likely single or tandem seat, with no visible canopy.
